executive_summary: Child A (dependency root) of orchestrator SD-LEO-INFRA-BREAKAGE-DETECTOR-SURFACE-001: freeze the enumerated break-class taxonomy that is the X2 measurement DENOMINATOR (no post-hoc redefinition), plus the alert-encoding contract the 5 sibling children (B-F) consume. Create lib/coordinator/break-class-taxonomy.cjs as the SINGLE source of truth, mirroring the proven lib/coordinator/row-growth.cjs pattern (CommonJS, Object.freeze, pure, require()-able). The FROZEN CONTRACT (orchestrator-owned, do NOT re-litigate): the canonical alert encoding is system_alerts.metadata.break_class; alert_type is written via toAlertType() to a value the LIVE system_alerts_alert_type_check admits (circuit_breaker/threshold_breach/system_health/eva_error) — so NO alert_type CHECK-widening migration (which would risk the 3 live EVA SECURITY-DEFINER functions). Severity uses the live system_alerts 3-level vocab {info,warning,critical}. A pinning test (tests/unit/break-class-taxonomy.test.js) IS the freeze: exact-array literal, frozen, severity-legal, toAlertType-legal, version-pinned. ~115 LOC.

functional_requirements.title: lib/coordinator/break-class-taxonomy.cjs — single source of truth
functional_requirements.description: CommonJS module mirroring lib/coordinator/row-growth.cjs (Object.freeze, pure, require()-able by detector scripts + the canary + the harness). Exports: frozen ordered BREAK_CLASSES (7 ids, stable order): schema-drift, migration-fail, row-growth-anomaly, RLS-regression, model-availability-cap (slash-normalized), gate-pipeline-down, payment-webhook-fail. BREAK_CLASS_SPEC: frozen map keyed by id -> { label, defaultSeverity (in {info,warning,critical}), detector (producing component, aspirational mapping not a readiness claim) }. toAlertType(breakClassId): returns a legal system_alerts.alert_type (the canonical encoding carries the class id in metadata.break_class; alert_type is one of circuit_breaker/threshold_breach/system_health/eva_error). DENOMINATOR_COUNT = 7. TAXONOMY_VERSION (pinned string).

functional_requirements.title: tests/unit/break-class-taxonomy.test.js — the freeze
functional_requirements.description: vitest unit pins (loaded via createRequire like row-growth-detector.test.js): (1) BREAK_CLASSES deep-equals the exact 7-element array in exact order (hard-coded literal — any add/remove/reorder fails); (2) BREAK_CLASSES.length === DENOMINATOR_COUNT === 7; (3) BREAK_CLASSES + BREAK_CLASS_SPEC are frozen (Object.isFrozen true); (4) every id has a spec entry and vice-versa (no orphans); (5) every defaultSeverity is in {info,warning,critical}; (6) toAlertType() of every class returns a value in the legal alert_type set; (7) TAXONOMY_VERSION pinned to an exact literal.

functional_requirements.title: Stable contract surface for sibling children B-F
functional_requirements.description: The module's exported API IS the cross-child contract the orchestrator froze: BREAK_CLASSES (iteration order), BREAK_CLASS_SPEC (per-class label/defaultSeverity/detector), toAlertType() (break-class -> legal alert_type), DENOMINATOR_COUNT (the X2 catch-rate denominator the child-F harness imports), TAXONOMY_VERSION. Document these as the stable public surface (JSDoc) so child B (recordSystemAlert writer), C (detector wiring), D (canary), E (board), and F (harness) all consume ONE source — no re-declaration of the class list, severity, or encoding anywhere else. This is the writer<=reader-parity guarantee for the whole pack.

technical_requirements.description: Mirror lib/coordinator/row-growth.cjs EXACTLY in shape: CommonJS, Object.freeze on all exported consts, pure (no DB/IO), module.exports. The detector-family doctrine (lib/coordinator/detectors.cjs): pure functions over injected data, .cjs convention, require()-able.

Severity vocab = the live system_alerts {info,warning,critical} (NOT eva_severity {critical,high,medium,low}). toAlertType() must return only values in the live system_alerts_alert_type_check set; class id rides in metadata.break_class. NO alert_type CHECK widening (out of scope — protects the 3 live EVA SECURITY-DEFINER fns).

Identifier hygiene: 'model-availability/cap' is normalized to the slash-free id 'model-availability-cap' (valid identifier, safe as a jsonb key / board filter); human label kept in BREAK_CLASS_SPEC.label.

test_scenarios.expected: all 7 pin groups pass; the exact-array literal + frozen + version pins make the denominator un-redefinable
returns a value in {circuit_breaker, threshold_breach, system_health, eva_error} — the live alert_type CHECK set

acceptance_criteria: lib/coordinator/break-class-taxonomy.cjs exists, is require()-able, exports frozen BREAK_CLASSES(7) + BREAK_CLASS_SPEC + toAlertType() + DENOMINATOR_COUNT(7) + TAXONOMY_VERSION
The taxonomy is the X2 denominator and is pinned by test (no post-hoc redefinition possible without a deliberate, reviewed test change)
Every defaultSeverity is legal for system_alerts; toAlertType() returns only legal alert_type values; NO alert_type CHECK widening
Sibling children B-F can import the contract from this module

risks.risk: alert_type CHECK violation if a class id is written directly into alert_type
risks.mitigation: toAlertType() maps to a legal value; class id rides in metadata.break_class; pinned by test #6
BREAK_CLASS_SPEC.detector documented as aspirational mapping, not a readiness claim (detectors arrive in children C/D)
